_readme: Choose ONE of the three mcpServers blocks below based on your setup.
Delete the other two blocks and rename your chosen one to 'mcpServers'.
Never commit real passwords — keep this file as a template only.
Option A: mcp-remote (recommended) — server runs on a remote/VM host.
Option B: Local Python — server runs on the same machine as Claude Desktop.
Option C: Docker — server runs via Docker on the same machine.

_comment: Use this when the MCP server runs on a separate Linux host (e.g. your Wazuh server or Vagrant VM).
The server must be running as a systemd service on the remote host (see README).
Replace WAZUH_SERVER_IP with the IP of your Wazuh host.
Use this when Claude Desktop and the MCP server run on the same machine.
Prerequisites: pip install -e . inside the wazuh-mcp directory.
Replace all <PLACEHOLDER> values with your actual values.
Use this when running the MCP server via Docker on the same machine as Claude Desktop.
Prerequisites: docker compose build inside the wazuh-mcp directory.
The .env file must exist at the path specified in --env-file.
Replace /absolute/path/to/wazuh-mcp with the actual path on your machine.
